oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_record_object_behavior.json.description: Returned records support key-based field access and equality based on the represented row.

feature11_delegated_record_behavior.json.description: A returned record can delegate model-defined behavior and preserve argument and callback values.

cases.expected_output: {"value":"Foo - All about Widget A - foo - bar"}

feature1_relational_queries.json.description: Relational queries return ordered lightweight records, selected fields, first matching records, and normalized not-found errors.

cases.expected_output: {"username":"bob","sql":["root: SELECT users.* FROM users WHERE users.username = 'bob' LIMIT 1"]}

feature2_raw_sql_parameters.json.description: SQL text inputs with supported placeholder styles bind scalar and list parameters before returning records.

cases.expected_output: {"records":["Widget A","Widget B","Widget C","Widget D","Widget E","Widget F","Widget G"]}

feature3_scalar_projection.json.description: Column projections return scalar arrays or row arrays instead of record objects.

cases.expected_output: {"values":["Widget A","Widget B","Widget C","Widget D","Widget E","Widget F","Widget G"]}
{"values":[["Widget A",8],["Widget B",8],["Widget C",8],["Widget D",8],["Widget E",8],["Widget F",8],["Widget G",8]]}
{"values":[["Widget A",8],["Widget B",8],["Widget C",8],["Widget D",8],["Widget E",8],["Widget F",8],["Widget G",8]]}

feature4_batched_iteration.json.description: Large result sets can be consumed in fixed-size batches while preserving ordering, offsets, limits, and loaded relationships.

cases.expected_output: {"batch_count":3,"batches":[["Widget A","Widget B","Widget C"],["Widget D","Widget E","Widget F"],["Widget G"]],"flattened":["Widget A","Widget B","Widget C","Widget D","Widget E","Widget F","Widget G"]}

{"records":[{"name":"Widget B","category":"Foo","detail":"All about Widget B","line_items":[]},{"name":"Widget C","category":"Foo","detail":"All about Widget C","line_items":[{"amount":200,"order_total":520}]},{"name":"Widget D","category":"Bar","detail":"All about Widget D","line_items":[{"amount":300,"order_total":520}]}]}

feature5_direct_relationship_loading.jso.description: Records can include directly related one-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-one, and join-table collections.

cases.expected_output: {"records":[{"widget":"Widget A","category":"Foo"},{"widget":"Widget B","category":"Foo"},{"widget":"Widget C","category":"Foo"},{"widget":"Widget D","category":"Bar"},{"widget":"Widget E","category":"Bar"},{"widget":"Widget F","category":"Bar"},{"widget":"Widget G","category":"Bar"}]}

{"records":[{"widget":"Widget A","detail":"All about Widget A"},{"widget":"Widget B","detail":"All about Widget B"},{"widget":"Widget C","detail":"All about Widget C"},{"widget":"Widget D","detail":"All about Widget D"},{"widget":"Widget E","detail":"All about Widget E"},{"widget":"Widget F","detail":"All about Widget F"},{"widget":"Widget G","detail":"All about Widget G"}]}

feature6_indirect_relationship_loading.j.description: Records can include relationships reached through intermediate records, with optional optimized intermediate projections and filters.

cases.expected_output: {"records":[{"customer":"Jane","categories":["Bar","Foo"]},{"customer":"Jon","categories":["Foo"]}],"sql":["root: SELECT customers.* FROM customers ORDER BY customers.name ASC","root.through(orders): SELECT orders.* FROM orders WHERE orders.customer_id IN (846114006, 980204181)","root.through(orders).through(line_items): SELECT line_items.* FROM line_items WHERE line_items.order_id IN (683130438, 834596858)","root.through(orders).through(line_items).categories: SELECT categories.* FROM categories WHERE categories.id IN (208889123, 922717355)"]}

{"records":[{"customer":"Jane","categories":["Bar","Foo"]},{"customer":"Jon","categories":["Foo"]}],"sql":["root: SELECT customers.* FROM customers ORDER BY customers.name ASC","root.through(orders): SELECT id, customer_id FROM orders WHERE orders.customer_id IN (846114006, 980204181)","root.through(orders).through(line_items): SELECT id, order_id, category_id FROM line_items WHERE line_items.order_id IN (683130438, 834596858)","root.through(orders).through(line_items).categories: SELECT categories.* FROM categories WHERE categories.id IN (208889123, 922717355)"]}

feature7_polymorphic_relationship_loadin.description: A related record slot whose target table varies per row resolves each row to the correct target record.

feature8_missing_data_errors.json.description: Accessing unloaded relationships, unselected fields, or undefined related members reports normalized black-box errors with the affected path.

feature9_value_casting.json.description: Database values are exposed with query-layer boolean status, coded status, and local-time representations.

_constraints.output_rule: Returns a JSON object with a "records" array containing groups sorted alphabetically.
Returns a JSON object with a "rows" array containing scalar fields explicitly requested.

_constraints.input_condition: "request" is "first_account_by_login" AND "required" is false OR not provided AND match exists
_constraints.output_rule: Returns a JSON object with the match fields and the SQL query string in an array.

_constraints.input_condition: "request" is "first_account_by_login" AND "required" is true AND match does not exist
_constraints.output_rule: Returns a JSON object with a single "error" key set to "not_found".

_constraints.input_condition: "request" is in ["sql_items_by_group", "sql_items_all_groups"] AND parameters are scalar or list
_constraints.output_rule: Executes SQL via placeholder binding and returns results in "records" array.
Uses %style placeholders and binds array values correctly.
Uses named style placeholders and binds array values correctly.
Uses ? style placeholders and binds array values correctly.
Returns "values" array: flat array for single column, array of arrays for multi-column.
Computes length of strings and returns scalar values preserving row order.
Returns "batch_count", "batches" (array of arrays), and "flattened" (single array).

_constraints.input_condition: "request" is "batch_items_offset_limit" AND either "offset" or "limit" is defined
_constraints.output_rule: Returns a "records" array applying pagination before batching, ignoring batch sizes if pagination is active.
Returns records with base fields and already-loaded related data.
Returns base records with related category values included.
Returns base records with related detail description.
Returns base records with an array of child amounts.
Returns records with related collection in an array.
Returns records with full traversal trace including all columns from intermediate steps.
Returns records with optimized intermediate queries (keys only) in "sql" trace.
Filters final related groups based on a condition (implied: only 'Foo').
Resolves each row to the correct target name dynamically.
Returns error with specific association name in the object.
Returns error for missing column access.
Returns error for missing column in nested relationship path.
Returns error for navigating to an undefined member.
Returns boolean values correctly mapped (true/false) alongside string data.
Returns array of human-readable labels corresponding to numeric codes.
Returns timestamp in specific localized format.
Returns all records as an object where keys are field names.
Returns boolean indicating if two record objects are the same identity instance.
Delegates method execution on the record object.
Appends argument value to the delegated output.
exact_values: Foo - All about Widget A - foo

_constraints.output_rule: Appends block value to the delegated output.
